title: Dry Sherry Dumplings
description: A spicy and dense dish featuring dry sherry, perfect for a monsoon Ukrainian meal that's both comforting and light.

ingredients.name: Dried sherry
ingredients.notes: Choose a dry sherry that complements the dish's spiciness

instructions: Mix pork mince with chopped onion, minced garlic, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Gradually add dried sherry while mixing to prevent clumping. Cook over medium heat for about 10 minutes or until the mixture thickens slightly.
In a separate pan, lightly fry cherry tomatoes in a bit of oil until they start to soften and release their juices.

Divide the pork mixture into small portions. On each portion, spread a thin layer of flour mixture. Place a cherry tomato half on one side of the filling, fold over from the opposite side, seal well, and pinch off any excess dough.
Roll out small amounts of dough until they are about 3-4cm in diameter. Gently place dumplings onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm with a sprinkle of fresh herbs and extra sherry on the side.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
